content:
# New Starter First Day Checklist

**Tool ID:** T067-01
**Block:** B067 (New Starter Induction)
**Domain:** Team & Talent (D04_team_talent)
**Level:** L1
**Format:** Checklist
**Role:** Manager
**Time Required:** Throughout first day (checklist takes 5 min to review)
**Version:** 1.0

---
## Welcome

This is a foundational tool -- the kind that prevents problems before they start. A new team member's first day shapes their entire relationship with your cafe. Get it right and they stay, learn fast, and become part of the team. Get it wrong and they leave within a month.

Not another tick-box exercise. This checklist exists because the details it covers are the ones that slip when you are busy -- and the ones that matter most when they do.

**Built for Manager working in Team & Talent.**
Use it. Adapt it. Make it yours.

---

## Purpose

Comprehensive first-day checklist covering right-to-work verification, contract signing, venue tour, safety briefing, team introductions, policy review, and equipment allocation. Signed by new starter and manager.

This tool supports **New Starter Induction** (B067) and is designed to be practical, printable, and immediately useful in a working cafe environment.

---

## How to Use This Tool

**Who:** Manager (completing checklist), New Starter (experiencing the induction)
**Time Required:** Items spread across the full first day. Checklist review: 5 minutes.
**Frequency:** Every new starter, every time. No shortcuts for "experienced" hires.

**What You Will Need:**
- This checklist (printed -- one copy for the file, one for the new starter)
- Employment contract (2 copies)
- Staff handbook / house rules document
- Uniform / apron
- Locker key or code
- Name badge
- Till/POS login credentials
- Food hygiene training booking confirmation

**Steps:**
1. Prepare everything on this list BEFORE the new starter arrives (ideally the day before)
2. Greet them personally at the door -- do not leave them standing at the counter
3. Work through the checklist in order throughout the day
4. Initial each item as completed
5. At the end of the day, sit down for 10 minutes and ask: "How was today? Any questions?"
6. Both sign off. File the completed checklist in the staff folder.

---

## Checklist

### Before They Arrive (Manager prep -- day before)

| # | Task | Done | Notes |
|---|------|------|-------|
| 0.1 | Contract printed (2 copies) and ready for signing | | |
| 0.2 | Uniform/apron clean and in correct size | | |
| 0.3 | Locker assigned and key/code ready | | |
| 0.4 | Name badge prepared | | |
| 0.5 | POS/till login created | | |
| 0.6 | Team informed: "New starter [Name] joining us tomorrow in [Role]" | | |
| 0.7 | Buddy assigned for first week: _____ | | |
| 0.8 | Food hygiene training booked (date: _____) | | |
| 0.9 | First week schedule printed and shared | | |

### Section A: Arrival and Administration (First 30 minutes)

| # | Task | Done | Initials | Notes |
|---|------|------|----------|-------|
| A1 | Greeted personally by Manager at the door | | | |
| A2 | Right-to-work documents checked and copied (passport / ID / work permit) | | | Document type: _____ |
| A3 | Employment contract reviewed and signed (both copies) | | | |
| A4 | Emergency contact details collected | | | |
| A5 | Bank details collected for payroll (or Sodra registration confirmed) | | | |
| A6 | Staff handbook / house rules reviewed and acknowledged | | | |
| A7 | Working hours, breaks, and pay date confirmed | | | |
| A8 | Uniform / apron issued | | | Size: _____ |
| A9 | Locker allocated and key/code given | | | Locker #: _____ |
| A10 | Name badge provided | | | |

### Section B: Safety Briefing (15 minutes)

| # | Task | Done | Initials | Notes |
|---|------|------|----------|-------|
| B1 | Fire exits shown (point to each one physically) | | | |
| B2 | Fire extinguisher and fire blanket locations shown | | | |
| B3 | Assembly point explained | | | Location: _____ |
| B4 | First aid kit location shown | | | |
| B5 | First aider on shift identified: _____ | | | |
| B6 | Accident book location shown and explained | | | |
| B7 | Slip/trip hazards discussed (wet floors, cables, step at _____) | | | |
| B8 | Manual handling basics (milk crates, coffee bags: lift with legs) | | | |
| B9 | Burns prevention (steam wand, hot water, oven -- if applicable) | | | |
| B10 | What to do in an emergency: "Call 112, then tell the Shift Lead" | | | |

### Section C: Venue Tour (20 minutes)

| # | Area | Shown | Notes |
|---|------|-------|-------|
| C1 | Front of house -- counter, seating areas, customer toilets | | |
| C2 | Espresso bar -- machine, grinder, knock box, scales | | |
| C3 | Back of house -- kitchen/prep area, wash-up station | | |
| C4 | Storage -- dry store, fridges, freezer (temperature logging location) | | |
| C5 | Cleaning supplies -- where chemicals are stored, COSHH sheets location | | |
| C6 | Staff area -- lockers, staff toilet, break area | | |
| C7 | Waste and recycling -- where each bin type goes, collection schedule | | |
| C8 | Delivery entrance -- where suppliers arrive, who checks deliveries | | |
| C9 | Office / admin area (if applicable) | | |

### Section D: Team Introductions

| # | Task | Done | Notes |
|---|------|------|-------|
| D1 | Introduced to every team member on shift by name and role | | |
| D2 | Buddy introduced and role explained: "_____ will be your go-to person this week" | | |
| D3 | Shift Lead / Head Barista introduced | | |
| D4 | Owner introduced (if on-site) or explained when they will meet | | |

### Section E: Role-Specific Basics (First shift observation)

| # | Task | Done | Notes |
|---|------|------|-------|
| E1 | POS / till walkthrough -- basic transactions, payment methods | | |
| E2 | Menu overview -- key items, prices, popular orders | | |
| E3 | Allergen procedure explained: "Never guess. Always check the matrix. Ask if unsure." | | |
| E4 | Handwashing procedure demonstrated (when, how, where) | | |
| E5 | Food safety basics: temperature danger zone (5-63 deg C), date labels, FIFO | | |
| E6 | Coffee basics (if barista role): shown the machine, grinder, not expected to make drinks today | | |
| E7 | Cleaning responsibilities explained -- what, when, how | | |
| E8 | End-of-day / closing tasks overview (observation only today) | | |

### Section F: End-of-Day Check-In (10 minutes before they leave)

| # | Task | Done | Notes |
|---|------|------|-------|
| F1 | Sit down with the new starter away from the counter | | |
| F2 | Ask: "How was your first day?" -- listen actively | | |
| F3 | Ask: "Is there anything you are unsure about?" | | |
| F4 | Confirm tomorrow's shift time and what to bring | | |
| F5 | Remind them of their buddy: "If you need anything, ask _____" | | |
| F6 | Thank them for joining the team | | |

### Sign-Off

| Field | Details |
|-------|---------|
| New starter name | |
| Role | |
| Start date | |
| Manager name | |
| All sections completed? | Yes / No (if No, note what remains: _____) |
| New starter signature | |
| Manager signature | |
| Date | |

---

## Common Risks

### Risk 1: Throwing Them Straight Into Service

**Why it happens:** Short-staffed, busy day, "they said they have experience." New starter ends up on the till or bar with no orientation. They make mistakes, feel stressed, and consider quitting.
**How to prevent:** Day 1 is ALWAYS observation and orientation. No solo service tasks. Even experienced hires need to learn YOUR way of doing things.
**If it is already happening:** Pull them off service now. Assign them to shadow their buddy. Complete the remaining checklist items.

### Risk 2: Paperwork Not Done on Day 1

**Why it happens:** Manager is busy, plans to "sort it later." Contract unsigned. Right-to-work not checked. Weeks pass. This is both a legal risk and a bad first impression.
**How to prevent:** Section A is completed in the first 30 minutes. No exceptions. Prepare everything the day before.
**If it is already happening:** Stop and complete Section A now. Backdate nothing -- document the actual date and note any delay.

### Risk 3: No Buddy Assigned

**Why it happens:** Manager assumes the team will "look after" the new person. But everyone is busy, and the new starter spends the day hovering awkwardly, not knowing who to ask.
**How to prevent:** Assign a named buddy before day 1. Brief the buddy: "Your job is to make [Name] feel welcome and answer their questions."
**If it is already happening:** Assign a buddy now. Even mid-shift, the buddy system works. Pick the most approachable team member.

---

## Customisation Notes

**This tool is a starting point, not a rulebook.**

**Independent / Specialty Cafe:** Add a "coffee philosophy" introduction -- share your approach to quality, sourcing, and what makes your cafe special. First impressions of culture matter.
**Chain / Franchise:** Add brand induction requirements (e-learning modules, brand values video, uniform standards). Use this as the practical day-1 supplement.
**Kiosk / Mobile:** Simplify venue tour. Focus on Section A (admin), B (safety), and E (role basics). Space constraints mean everything is close.
**Bakery Cafe:** Add bakery-specific safety (oven burns, flour dust, heavy trays) to Section B. Include bakery tour in Section C.

**Making it yours:**
- Prepare a simple "Welcome Pack" folder: contract, handbook, schedule, menu, allergen matrix
- Take a team photo on day 1 (with permission) and add it to the staff board
- Send a short welcome message in the team group chat before their first day
- Use the end-of-day check-in (Section F) every day for the first week, not just day 1

The structure matters more than the specific fields. Keep the structure, change the content.

---

## Connected Resources

**Other tools in this block:**
- T067-02 -- Induction Training Schedule (L2, template)
- T067-03 -- Buddy Mentor Guide (L2, SOP)
- T067-04 -- Induction Record and Acknowledgement Form (L2, template)
- T067-05 -- New Starter Feedback Survey (L3, template)

**Related blocks:**
- B068

**Parent block:** B067 -- New Starter Induction
**Module:** M04: Recruitment Onboarding & Development
---

*Tool Version 1.0 -- OIS Cafe v2*
*Built for cafe operators who value clarity, consistency, and craft.*
